comments_1: Unsolicited phone call to Saks' CEO expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.
CEO met with senior representatives of Sponsor A; no specific proposal or transaction terms were discussed.
Sadove met Richard Baker at Baker's request to discuss a potential acquisition of Saks; no specific proposal or transaction terms were discussed.
Indicated it was considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15.00 per share, in cash.
Indicated it was considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15.00 per share, in cash.
comments_2: Sponsor E participated in the meeting with Sponsor A.

comments_1: Sponsor A and Sponsor E were considering participating in a potential joint acquisition of Saks.
Sponsor A and Sponsor E were considering participating in a potential joint acquisition of Saks.
Preliminarily prepared to proceed with submission of a proposal at an indicative price range of $15.00 to $15.25 per share.
Preliminarily prepared to proceed with submission of a proposal at an indicative price range of $15.00 to $16.00 per share.
Board authorized implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with one of the potential acquirors that had executed a confidentiality agreement could be reached.
During the week of June 10, 2013, Saks was informed that Company F had indicated interest in participating with Sponsor A and Sponsor E in a potential acquisition of Saks.
comments_2: Company F engaged in due diligence of Saks, but no meetings were held between representatives of Saks and representatives of Company F.
comments_3: Company F did not participate in the offer ultimately submitted by Sponsor A and Sponsor G.

comments_1: Goldman Sachs distributed a draft merger agreement and process details to Hudson's Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E, requesting offers for an all-cash acquisition plus comments on the draft merger agreement by 07/11/2013.
Sponsor E informed Saks that Sponsor A was no longer intending to be a primary participant and that Sponsor E had entered discussions with Sponsor G regarding a potential joint acquisition of Saks.
Proposal included a revised draft merger agreement.
comments_2: Included information and documentation relating to committed debt and equity financing.

comments_1: Joint proposal stated the parties would require several weeks of additional due diligence to submit a more definitive proposal.
comments_2: Did not include a revised draft merger agreement or financing support documents.

comments_1: Initial formal offers were received from Hudson's Bay and from Sponsor E together with Sponsor G.
After the 07/11/2013 proposal, Saks was informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process.
comments_2: Sponsor E was again joined in its proposal by Sponsor A as a primary participant.

comments_1: Saks received a letter from Company H, a privately held U.S. company unknown to Saks and its advisors, purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash, with no details or further information.
comments_2: Goldman Sachs subsequently attempted on more than one occasion to contact the appropriate person at Company H by telephone and e-mail but was unsuccessful.
comments_3: Neither Saks nor Goldman Sachs received any subsequent communications from Company H.

comments_1: In the evening of July 23, 2013, there was no indication from Sponsor A or Sponsor E that they were prepared to increase their indicated price beyond their initially indicated range of $14.50 to $15.50 per share.
comments_2: During the week of July 22, Sponsor A and Sponsor E indicated a willingness to continue to pursue a transaction based on a valuation above $15 per share but believed that it would be unlikely that they would achieve a value above the top end of their indicated range of $14.50 to $15.50 per share.
comments_3: It also did not appear likely that Sponsor A and Sponsor E would be prepared to conclude their diligence more quickly than they had previously indicated.

comments_1: Subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, Hudson's Bay advised it was prepared to offer $16.00 per share.
comments_2: Hudson's Bay was agreeable to Saks' position on substantially all significant issues in the draft merger agreement and financing documents.

comments_1: Saks, Hudson's Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement later on July 28, 2013.
Saks and Hudson's Bay issued a joint press release announcing entry into the transaction.
Approximate date based on Saks' later statement that Company I did not sign a confidentiality agreement until approximately two weeks after the merger agreement was executed.
comments_2: Company I conducted a due diligence investigation of Saks during the go-shop process.

comments_1: Go-shop period ended; no party was designated as an excluded party.
comments_2: Goldman Sachs contacted 58 potentially interested third parties; six expressed interest; only Company I signed a confidentiality agreement.
comments_3: No party contacted during the go-shop process submitted an acquisition proposal.
